metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: run_backup() depth 8 — full backup orchestration: dry-run vs real, versioned vs snapshot, progress wrapping, mode branching. Genuine 8-level conditional chain, all paths are necessary.
scan_files() depth 7 — tree walking with dual code paths (with/without Rich progress). Both modes need identical walk logic with different UI feedback.
cleanup_deleted_files() depth 8 — three-pass deletion strategy (dirs, files, empty dirs) each with dry-run branching and state tracking between passes.
copy_versioned_file() depth 8 — baseline creation + diff generation + retry logic + permission management via temporarily_writable context managers.
SKIP — file is being rewritten. handle_command() depth 5.
SKIP — file is being rewritten. 6 functions flagged (depth 4-5).
operations/ is a business domain for backup — groups all file manipulation handlers (copy, scan, cleanup, path building). Renaming to files/ would break 6+ import chains across modules and handlers.
operations/ is a business domain for backup — groups all file manipulation handlers. Renaming would break import chains.
operations/ is a business domain for backup — groups all file manipulation handlers. Renaming would break import chains.
operations/ is a business domain for backup — groups all file manipulation handlers. Renaming would break import chains.
operations/ groups both local file ops and Drive sync ops — two business domains sharing a common operational layer. Splitting would duplicate shared patterns.

929 lines: single Google Drive API client class — auth, folder management, upload, file tracking, sync orchestration. All methods are tightly coupled to the Drive service instance. Splitting the class would break the shared state (auth tokens, tracker cache, thread-local services). Also: operations/ directory naming is architectural.
operations/ groups Drive test ops alongside other operational handlers. Renaming would break imports.

utils/ contains cross-cutting utilities (safe_print, temporarily_writable, ensure_backup_directory) used by 5+ files across multiple handler domains. Genuinely cross-domain — no single business domain owns these.
utils/ contains cross-cutting utilities. backup_timestamps is used by both backup_core and google_drive_sync — belongs to no single domain.
Imports BackupResult model from models/ — handlers legitimately share data models. Models are the shared contract between handlers.
Imports BackupResult model from models/ — handlers legitimately share data models.
Imports safe_print from utils/ — cross-cutting utility needed for console output. utils/ exists to serve all handler domains.
Imports temporarily_writable and safe_print from utils/ — cross-cutting utilities for file permission management and console output.
Imports from json/drive_sync_json — Drive ops handler needs Drive JSON persistence. These are tightly coupled by business domain (Google Drive sync).
Imports temporarily_writable and safe_print from utils/ — cross-cutting utilities for permission management and console output.
Imports from json/drive_sync_json — Drive client needs JSON persistence for config, data, and log files. Tightly coupled by business domain.
Imports BackupResult model from models/ — report formatting needs the result data model. Models are the shared contract between handlers.
GoogleDriveSync is a class name (PascalCase correct). _clear_file_tracker_handler and get_file_tracker_stats are functions, not constants — seedgo false positive on module-level function references.
log_entry is a local variable inside log_operation(), not a module-level constant. PEP 8: local vars use snake_case.
Renaming diff_generator.py → generator.py would make the file name ambiguous across the codebase. The diff_ prefix clarifies the file's domain when referenced in imports and logs.
Renaming config_handler.py → handler.py would make the file name ambiguous. The config_ prefix clarifies domain in imports (from ...config.config_handler).
build, MediaFileUpload, Request are third-party imports from Google API libraries. Cannot rename external library symbols.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
